channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iraq says $19M found in hideouts linked to ex-oil minister
item.description: Iraqi authorities say they seized the equivalent of $19 million in cash and several kilograms of gold jewelry

item.title: Ebola spreading more quickly in DRC, while Uganda is close to being virus-free
item.description: Uganda ⁠⁠discharges last-remaining patient, as WHO says Ebola has 'expanded faster than any previous outbreak' in DRC.

item.title: What could a US ground invasion of Iran look like?
item.description: Donald Trump has refused to rule out sending US troops into Iran as fighting resumes and US strikes continue.

item.title: Iran condemns ‘barbaric’ US attack near children’s cancer hospital
item.description: Iran accuses US of 'cowardly war crime' as doctor says blast was 'so close' they thought the hospital was struck.

item.title: WHO: Ebola spreading faster than ever recorded in DR Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization warns the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo is spreading faster than any previous outbreak.

item.title: In Syria’s Jobar, locals struggle to rebuild their destroyed homes
item.description: Jobar was held by rebels for much of Syria's 13-year war but was destroyed by repeated government assaults.

item.title: US approves nearly $2bn in weapons sale to Saudi Arabia
item.description: The sale intends to bolster Saudi air defence systems as the kingdom risks escalation with the Iran-backed Houthis.

item.title: Rare protests in Kyiv over dismissal of defence minister
item.description: Rare protests erupted in central Kyiv over Ukrainian President Zelenskyy’s dismissal of defence minister.

item.title: Eleven killed in Algeria orphanage fire
item.description: Eleven people were killed and 19 injured after a fire tore through an orphanage in Algiers.

item.title: Ex-CEO of Italian motorway sentenced to 12 years for Genoa bridge collapse
item.description: In all, 32 convicted and 25 acquitted or cleared by statute ‌of limitations over 2018 Morandi bridge disaster.

item.title: US to impose new 25 percent tariffs on some Brazilian imports
item.description: Beef, coffee and rare-earth materials escaped US tariffs amid trade tensions with Brazil over alleged unfair practices.

item.title: Trump limits length of visas for students, exchange visitors, journalists
item.description: New rule ends open-ended stays and requires extensions after fixed visa periods for students and foreign media.

item.title: How can Argentina and Messi beat Spain and Yamal to defend World Cup?
item.description: Despite Lionel Messi's presence, Argentina enter the 2026 final as underdogs to Spain and their starlet Lamine Yamal.

item.title: Vance says Israeli campaign tried to sway US opinion against Iran diplomacy
item.description: The US vice-president also said he faced 'vicious' personal attacks over his diplomatic outreach to end war.

item.title: Israeli attacks kill at least five Palestinians in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
item.description: Attacks continue in violation of the October agreement with more than 1,100 Palestinians killed since then.

item.title: Hamas leadership run-off expected between Meshaal and al-Hayya
item.description: Israeli assassinations of several key leaders force Hamas to find new head, a process expected to be completed next week

item.title: Air defences intercept drones over Erbil, Iraq
item.description: Verified eyewitness video captures multiple aerial interceptions over Erbil, Iraq

item.title: UK urges FIFA to investigate Argentina over World Cup Falklands banner
item.description: The United Kingdom and Argentina fought a brief war over the British overseas territory in 1982.

item.title: What happens if Mitch McConnell is unable to finish his US Senate term?
item.description: The longtime party leader's absence from the Senate is likely to have repercussions for the Republican agenda.

item.title: Data breach reportedly targets India’s Kudankulam nuclear power plant
item.description: Purported blueprints of parts of India's largest plant exposed by World Leaks ransomware group, Reuters reports.

item.title: Has Bali’s Melukat ritual turned into a commodified religious ceremony?
item.description: With the world in chaos, many travellers are seeking healing experiences during the summer holidays.

item.title: World Cup: Why is there a third-place playoff between France and England?
item.description: When was it introduced, does it have an impact on the Golden Boot race and what do the managers think?

item.title: How World Cup football jerseys became political fashion in Latin America
item.description: In both Brazil and Colombia, far-right political movements have coopted the national football team's jersey. But why?

item.title: Epstein linked to Israeli ‘deep state’, US intel, says Vance: What we know
item.description: In podcast interview, Vance also acknowledges Trump administration 'screwed up' on communications around Epstein files.

item.title: US or Iran — Who needs a new truce deal more?
item.description: Both sides face pressure points that could make a protracted war painful.
